prompt: Write a concise benchmark note for Nate. Cover speed, memory pressure, correctness risk, and the next engineering step. Keep it blunt and specific.

rejected_layer_sharded_lru.verdict: No material hit/read benefit over the simpler global LRU; not retained in runtime code.

verdict.ship: Keep the bounded global packed ExpertBank LRU as an opt-in direct/hybrid decode optimization.
verdict.default: Off. Dense remains the default q4 mode and packed cache defaults to 0 GiB.
verdict.correctness: Cache-on preserved the exact four greedy token ids and repeated identical daemon requests were byte-identical.
verdict.performance: Passed the decode gate with 24.1% lower median decode step and improved total step wall by 10.3%. Aggregate read reduction was 8.0%, below the initial 15% estimate because prompt-prefill route overlap is limited.
verdict.memory: Swap delta remained 0.0 GiB and dense+packed cache budgets are jointly capped at 16 GiB.
